mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect aggregate income expansion that drives corporate revenue expectations and retail investor equity demand, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 5.7% in Jan 2015 shows recovering labor market
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases month-over-month but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25% sustains a yield-seeking environment where compressed risk-free returns redirect institutional capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 0.11% in Jan 2015 indicates ongoing zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and decline in the unemployment rate creates a dual-momentum signal where capacity utilization pressures boost pricing power and corporate profit margins, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% in Jan 2015 shows continued labor market improvement alongside industrial activity
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with tight labor markets, a contraction in retail sales signals consumer demand exhaustion that triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% in Jan 2015 indicates tight labor market conditions
failure_condition: RSAFS declines month-over-month while UNRATE is below 6% but SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation coinciding with contracting retail sales generates a stagflationary profit squeeze where rising input costs accompany weakening final demand, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at 236.1 shows mild inflationary pressure amid moderate retail activity
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while RSAFS declines, SPX 20-day forward returns average above zero over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1 million units signal broadening construction employment and durable goods demand that lifts cyclical corporate revenues, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1089 in Dec 2014 indicates elevated housing activity
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 1000 but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes broad-based earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.7% in Jan 2015 down from peak but vulnerable to reversal
failure_condition: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contractions in industrial production coincide with negative earnings guidance revisions in the goods-producing sector, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical exposure is repriced.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.5 shows manufacturing recovery levels
failure_condition: INDPRO declines month-over-month but SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months over a 2-year rolling window.
